body:
## Summary
- Moves `default_output_attributes`, `default_velocity_attributes`, and `default_tracer_attributes` method implementations from `OceananigansNCDatasetsExt` into `src/Models/output_attributes.jl`
- Keeps only a generic fallback `default_output_attributes(model) = Dict()` in `OutputWriters`
- Models defines specialized methods via dispatch on `NonhydrostaticModel`, `HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel`, and `ShallowWaterModel`
- `add_schedule_metadata!` methods remain in `OutputWriters` (no external type dependencies)

## Motivation
These functions don't depend on NCDatasets but were defined entirely inside the extension. This prevented downstream package extensions (e.g. `ClimaSeaIceNCDatasetsExt`) from adding methods at compile time, forcing them to use `__init__` + `@eval` which breaks incremental compilation on Julia 1.12.

Moving the methods to `Models` (following the existing pattern for `default_included_properties` and `checkpointer_address`) makes them available to downstream packages via standard `import`.

**Companion PR:** CliMA/ClimaSeaIce.jl — `ss/fix-netcdf-extension`

## Test plan
- [ ] Existing NetCDF writer tests pass
- [ ] ClimaSeaIce precompiles successfully with NumericalEarth on Julia 1.12
title: (0.106.4) Move NetCDF output attribute methods from extension to Models module

body:
## Summary
- Extend `_launch!` to handle `active_cells_map::NamedTuple` by iterating over all non-nothing sub-maps, supporting distributed grids with split active cell maps
- Add `get_active_cells_map(grid, Val(:xyz))` returning the full `grid.interior_active_cells` (single array for serial, NamedTuple for distributed, `nothing` for regular grids)
- Add `get_active_cells_map(grid, Val(:xy))` for active columns, replacing `get_active_column_map`
- Use active cells maps in all CATKE kernel launches: closure field computation, TKE diffusivity, TKE time-stepping (AB2 and SplitRK3), and surface buoyancy flux
- Migrate all existing `get_active_column_map` call sites (SplitExplicit, tests) to `get_active_cells_map(grid, Val(:xy))`
- Add test for CATKE with `ImmersedBoundaryGrid` + `active_cells_map=true`

## Test plan
- [x] Package loads
- [x] CATKE with ImmersedBoundaryGrid + active_cells_map time-steps correctly
- [x] Existing CATKE tests pass (regular grids, closure tuples, substepping)
- [x] SplitExplicit tests pass (uses migrated Val(:xy) API)
- [x] Active cells map tests pass
- [ ] Distributed simulations with split active cell maps

labels.description: runs preconfigured benchamarks and spits out timing
labels.name: benchmark performance

title: Use active_cells_map for CATKE on ImmersedBoundaryGrids
